heading: Reauthorization; reporting requirements
label: 379j–1 Reauthorization; reporting requirements
notes: Termination of SectionFor termination of section by , see Effective and Termination Dates note below.section 2007(b) of Pub. L. 117–180

L. 110–85section 379i of this title

children.heading: General requirements
children.label: i General requirements
children.text: Beginning with fiscal year 2023, for each fiscal year for which fees are collected under this subpart, the Secretary shall prepare and submit to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives annual reports concerning the progress of the Food and Drug Administration in achieving the goals identified in the letters described in section 201(b) of the Medical Device User Fee Amendments of 2022 during such fiscal year and the future plans of the Food and Drug Administration for meeting the goals.1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: I
children.text: the number of premarket applications filed under per fiscal year for each review division;section 360e of this title

children.label: II
children.text: the number of reports submitted under per fiscal year for each review division;section 360(k) of this title

children.label: III
children.text: the number of expedited development and priority review designations under section 360e–3 of this title per fiscal year;1

children.label: IV
children.text: the number of investigational device exemption applications submitted under per fiscal year, including for each review division; andsection 360j(g) of this title

children.label: V
children.text: the number of expedited development and priority review requests and designations under per fiscal year, including for each review division.section 360e–3 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Not later than 30 calendar days after the end of the second quarter of fiscal year 2023, and not later than 30 calendar days after the end of each quarter of each fiscal year thereafter, the Secretary shall post the data described in subclause (II) on the internet website of the Food and Drug Administration for such quarter and on a cumulative basis for such fiscal year, and may remove duplicative data from the annual report under this subparagraph.

children.label: aa
children.text: The number and titles of draft and final guidance on topics related to the process for the review of devices, and whether such guidances were issued as required by statute or pursuant to the letters described in section 201(b) of the Medical Device User Fee Amendments of 2022; and1

children.label: bb
children.text: The number and titles of public meetings held on topics related to the process for the review of devices, and if such meetings were required by statute or pursuant to a commitment under the letters described in section 201(b) of the Medical Device User Fee Amendments of 2022.1

children.heading: Rationale for MDUFA program changes
children.label: iv Rationale for MDUFA program changes
I
children.text: data, analysis, and discussion of the changes in the number of individuals hired as agreed upon in the letters described in section 2001(b) of the Medical Device User Fee Amendments of 2022 and the number of remaining vacancies, the number of full-time equivalents funded by fees collected pursuant to , and the number of full time equivalents funded by budget authority at the Food and Drug Administration by each division within the Center for Devices and Radiological Health, the Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research, the Office of Regulatory Affairs, and the Office of the Commissioner;section 379j of this title

children.label: aa
children.text: drivers of such changes; and

children.label: bb
children.text: changes in the average total cost per full-time equivalent in the medical device review program;

children.label: III
children.text: for each of the Center for Devices and Radiological Health, the Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research, the Office of Regulatory Affairs, and the Office of the Commissioner, the number of employees for whom time reporting is required and the number of employees for whom time reporting is not required; and

children.label: IV
children.text: data, analysis, and discussion of the changes in the average full-time equivalent hours required to complete review of medical device application types.

children.label: aa
children.text: the number of applications filed and reports submitted during one fiscal year for which a decision is not scheduled to be made until the following fiscal year; and

children.label: bb
children.text: the aggregate number of applications for each fiscal year that did not meet the goals as identified by the letters described in section 201(b) of the Medical Device User Fee Amendments of 2022 for the applicable fiscal year.1

children.label: II
children.text: Relevant data to determine whether the Center for Devices and Radiological Health has met performance enhancement goals identified by the letters described in section 201(b) of the Medical Device User Fee Amendments of 2022 for the applicable fiscal year.1

children.label: III
children.text: The most common causes and trends for external or other circumstances affecting the ability of the Center for Devices and Radiological Health, the Office of Regulatory Affairs, or the Food and Drug Administration to meet review time and performance enhancement goals identified by the letters described in section 201(b) of the Medical Device User Fee Amendments of 2022.1

children.heading: Publication
children.label: B Publication
children.text: With regard to information to be reported by the Food and Drug Administration to industry on a quarterly and annual basis pursuant to the letters described in section 201(b) of the Medical Device User Fee Amendments of 2022, the Secretary shall make such information publicly available on the Internet Web site of the Food and Drug Administration not later than 60 days after the end of each quarter or 120 days after the end of each fiscal year, respectively, to which such information applies. This information shall include the status of the independent assessment identified in the letters described in such section 201(b).1

children.heading: Updates
children.label: C Updates
children.text: The Secretary shall include in each report under subparagraph (A) information on all previous cohorts for which the Secretary has not given a complete response on all device premarket applications and reports, supplements, and premarket notifications in the cohort.

children.heading: Goals met
children.label: A Goals met
children.text: For each fiscal year, if the Secretary determines, based on the analysis under paragraph (1)(A)(iv), that each of the goals identified by the letters described in section 201(b) of the Medical Device User Fee Amendments of 2022 for the applicable fiscal year have been met, the corrective action report shall include recommendations on ways in which the Secretary can improve and streamline the medical device application review process.1

children.label: i
children.text: a justification for such determination;

children.label: ii
children.text: a description of the types of circumstances, in the aggregate, under which applications or reports submitted under or notifications submitted under missed the review goal times but were approved during the first cycle review, as applicable;section 360e of this titlesection 360(k) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: a summary and any trends with regard to the circumstances for which a review goal was missed; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the performance enhancement goals that were not achieved during the previous fiscal year and a description of efforts the Food and Drug Administration has put in place for the fiscal year in which the report is submitted to improve the ability of such agency to meet each such goal for the such fiscal year.2So in original.2

children.heading: Communications with Congress
children.label: A Communications with Congress
children.text: Each fiscal year, as applicable and requested, representatives from the Centers with expertise in the review of devices shall meet with representatives from the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives to report on the contents described in the reports under this section.

children.heading: Participation in congressional hearing
children.label: B Participation in congressional hearing
children.text: Each fiscal year, as applicable and requested, representatives from the Food and Drug Administration shall participate in a public hearing before the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives, to report on the contents described in the reports under this section. Such hearing shall occur not later than 120 days after the end of each fiscal year for which fees are collected under this subpart.

children.heading: Fiscal report
children.label: 4 Fiscal report
children.text: For fiscal years 2023 through 2027, not later than 120 days after the end of each fiscal year during which fees are collected under this subpart, the Secretary shall prepare and submit to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives, a report on the implementation of the authority for such fees during such fiscal year and the use, by the Food and Drug Administration, of the fees collected during such fiscal year for which the report is made.

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: 5 Public availability
children.text: The Secretary shall make the reports required under paragraphs (1) and (2) available to the public on the Internet Web site of the Food and Drug Administration.

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives;

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate;

children.label: C
children.text: scientific and academic experts;

children.label: D
children.text: health care professionals;

children.label: E
children.text: representatives of patient and consumer advocacy groups; and

children.label: F
children.text: the regulated industry.

children.label: A
children.text: publish a notice in the Federal Register requesting public input on the reauthorization;

children.label: B
children.text: hold a public meeting at which the public may present its views on the reauthorization, including specific suggestions for changes to the goals referred to in subsection (a)(1);

children.label: C
children.text: provide a period of 30 days after the public meeting to obtain written comments from the public suggesting changes to this subpart; and

children.label: D
children.text: publish the comments on the Food and Drug Administration’s Internet Web site.

children.heading: Periodic consultation
children.label: 3 Periodic consultation
children.text: Not less frequently than once every month during negotiations with the regulated industry, the Secretary shall hold discussions with representatives of patient and consumer advocacy groups to continue discussions of their views on the reauthorization and their suggestions for changes to this subpart as expressed under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Updates to Congress
children.label: 4 Updates to Congress
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with regulated industry, shall provide regular updates on negotiations on the reauthorization of this part to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives.

children.label: A
children.text: present the recommendations developed under paragraph (1) to the Congressional committees specified in such paragraph;

children.label: B
children.text: publish such recommendations in the Federal Register;

children.label: C
children.text: provide for a period of 30 days for the public to provide written comments on such recommendations;

children.label: D
children.text: hold a meeting at which the public may present its views on such recommendations; and

children.label: E
children.text: after consideration of such public views and comments, revise such recommendations as necessary.

children.heading: Transmittal of recommendations
children.label: 6 Transmittal of recommendations
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall transmit to Congress the revised recommendations under paragraph (4), a summary of the views and comments received under such paragraph, and any changes made to the recommendations in response to such views and comments.January 15, 2027

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: A Public availability
children.text: The Secretary shall make publicly available, on the public Web site of the Food and Drug Administration, minutes of all negotiation meetings conducted under this subsection between the Food and Drug Administration and the regulated industry, not later than 30 days after each such negotiation meeting.

children.heading: Content
children.label: B Content
children.text: The minutes described under subparagraph (A) shall summarize, in sufficient detail, any substantive proposal made by any party to the negotiations as well as significant controversies or differences of opinion during the negotiations and their resolution.
